intro: Youngia pygmaea, also known as the dwarf coneflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine, particularly in Chinese herbalism, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Youngia pygmaea include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Youngia pygmaea is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to promote health and alleviate symptoms of various diseases.
